skills.name: api-and-interface-design
skills.description: Guides stable API and interface design. Use when designing APIs, module boundaries, or any public interface.

skills.name: browser-testing-with-devtools
skills.description: Tests in real browsers via Chrome DevTools MCP. Use when building or debugging anything that runs in a browser.

skills.name: ci-cd-and-automation
skills.description: Automates CI/CD pipeline setup. Use when setting up or modifying build and deployment pipelines.

skills.name: code-review-and-quality
skills.description: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change.

skills.name: code-simplification
skills.description: Simplifies code for clarity. Use when refactoring code for clarity without changing behavior.

skills.name: context-engineering
skills.description: Optimizes agent context setup. Use when starting a new session, when agent output quality degrades, or when switching between tasks.

skills.name: debugging-and-error-recovery
skills.description: Guides systematic root-cause debugging. Use when tests fail, builds break, or behavior doesn't match expectations.

skills.name: deprecation-and-migration
skills.description: Manages deprecation and migration. Use when removing old systems, APIs, or features.

skills.name: documentation-and-adrs
skills.description: Records decisions and documentation. Use when making architectural decisions or changing public APIs.

skills.name: doubt-driven-development
skills.description: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review. Use when stakes are high or working in unfamiliar code.

skills.name: fresh-context-execution
skills.description: Execute all phases using fresh-context subagents to prevent context rot. Automatically parallelizes independent tasks. Use when running multi-step commands or when session context is degrading.

skills.name: frontend-ui-engineering
skills.description: Builds production-quality UIs. Use when building or modifying user-facing interfaces.

skills.name: git-workflow-and-versioning
skills.description: Structures git workflow practices. Use when making any code change, committing, or branching.

skills.name: idea-refine
skills.description: Refines raw ideas into sharp, actionable concepts. Use when an idea is still vague or needs stress-testing.

skills.name: incremental-implementation
skills.description: Delivers changes incrementally in thin vertical slices. Use when implementing any multi-file change or when tempted to write more than ~100 lines before testing.

skills.name: interview-me
skills.description: Extracts what the user actually wants through one-question-at-a-time interview. Use when an ask is underspecified.

skills.name: observability-and-instrumentation
skills.description: Instruments code so production behavior is visible. Use when adding logging, metrics, tracing, or alerting.

skills.name: performance-optimization
skills.description: Optimizes application performance. Use when performance requirements exist or Core Web Vitals need improvement.

skills.name: planning-and-task-breakdown
skills.description: Breaks work into ordered tasks. Use when you have a spec and need implementable task units.

skills.name: security-and-hardening
skills.description: Hardens code against vulnerabilities. Use when handling user input, authentication, or data storage.

skills.name: shipping-and-launch
skills.description: Prepares production launches. Use when preparing to deploy to production.

skills.name: skill-router
skills.description: Dynamically selects and loads the right skill based on user intent. Use when starting a task and unsure which skill to load.

skills.name: source-driven-development
skills.description: Grounds every implementation decision in official documentation. Use when building with frameworks where correctness matters.

skills.name: spec-driven-development
skills.description: Creates specs before coding. Use when starting a new project or feature and no specification exists yet.

skills.name: state-management
skills.description: Creates and maintains a shared state file that persists across sessions. Use when starting or resuming multi-step work.

skills.name: test-driven-development
skills.description: Drives development with tests. Use when implementing logic, fixing bugs, or changing behavior.

skills.name: using-agent-skills
skills.description: Discovers and invokes agent skills. Use when starting a session or deciding which skill applies.

skills.name: using-git-worktrees
skills.description: Creates isolated workspaces for parallel task execution. Use before fresh-context-execution when tasks can run concurrently.
